title: betula alba subsp. occidentalis
intro: Betula alba subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western white birch, is a subspecies of tree belonging to the family Betulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate colds. The health benefits of western white birch include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve stress, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Western white birch bark can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine as a tincture or in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
